title: Charred Light Malaysian Oats Roti
description: A light and cozy Malaysian dish featuring oats and ferment-kimchi, perfect for a healthy breakfast or snack. This recipe is Whole30-friendly and deliciously flavorful.
instructions: Heat a tava-griddle or non-stick pan over medium heat.
Mix oats, ferment-kimchi, coconut oil, salt, and black pepper in a bowl until well combined.
Add eggs to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Gradually add water to the dough and mix until a smooth consistency is achieved.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal portions, depending on desired roti size.
Roll out each portion into a thin circle, about 6-7 inches in diameter.
Place a roti on the tava-griddle and cook for 1-2 minutes, until the bottom is lightly charred.
Flip the roti and cook for another 1-2 minutes, until the other side is also lightly charred.
Repeat with the remaining dough portions.
